mnemonics.title: Path Functions Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: DJBEB - Directory (dirname), Join (join), Basename (basename), Exists (exists), Basename (basename)

mnemonics.title: Pathlib Attributes
mnemonics.content: NAME has SUFFIX, STEM has no suffix, PARENT is directory, PARENTS are ancestors, STAT gives details

acronyms.usage: Remember key os.path functions - though technically basename appears twice in mnemonic, it's memorable
Remember why pathlib is preferred - it provides portable path handling across OS
rglob searches recursively through all subdirectories

visualTips.visualization: Draw path as a tree: / (root) → home → user → file.txt. Parent shows 'above', name shows 'leaf', suffix shows 'last dot part'
Think of absolute as full street address, relative as 'turn left from here'. Absolute always starts from root (/ or C:\)

analogies.analogy: Like building a sandwich - you don't manually add separators, the join function adds the right 'condiments' (separators) automatically
Think of Path like a remote control for files - you point it at files and press buttons (methods) to do things
resolve() is like asking 'where exactly is this file on the map?' (following any shortcuts), absolute() is like asking 'what's the official address?' (may not follow symlinks)
